title: Lateral Movement Across Hosts
story: Threat hunting query flagged an unusual pattern: a service account that normally
only authenticates to a single host has been seen authenticating to multiple
hosts in sequence over a 30-minute window.
This pattern is consistent with lateral movement — an attacker using compromised
credentials to hop between systems, expanding access across the network.
Reconstruct the attack path and identify all pivoted hosts.

objectives.label: Identify the compromised service account used for pivoting
Identify the initial compromised host (first hop)
List the pivot sequence (comma-separated hostnames in order)
What was the final target host?

hints: Correlate auth logs across multiple hosts — look for svc_account logins
Sort events by time to reconstruct the movement sequence
Each hop comes from the PREVIOUS host's IP, not the attacker's original IP
Use: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case8_* svc_account | sort _time | table _time, host, _raw
splunk_queries: index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case8_* svc_account | sort _time | table _time, host, _raw
index=hex_lab sourcetype=hex_case8_* "Accepted" | rex field=_raw "for (?<user>\w+) from (?<src_ip>[\d.]+)" | table _time, host, user, src_ip | sort _time
